class.name: SemVer
class.description:
[SemVer] is a structured representation of a strict SemVer 2.0 version, broken into [member major], [member minor], [member patch], [member prerelease], and [member build_metadata] components. Use the static factory [method parse_semver] to parse a version string, [method create_semver] to build one from raw components, and the [method eq] / [method neq] / [method lt] / [method lte] / [method gt] / [method gte] methods to compare versions according to SemVer precedence rules. [SemVer] is the base class for [GodotVer]; if you are working with Godot engine version strings (which use a non-strict format like [code]"4.3.stable.official.77dcf97d8"[/code]), use [GodotVer] instead. [b]Note:[/b] following the SemVer 2.0 specification, [member build_metadata] is [i]not[/i] considered when ordering or comparing versions - two versions that differ only in their build metadata compare equal under [method eq]. [codeblock] var v = SemVer.parse_semver("4.2.0-rc.1+sha.md5.f9300dc53") print(v.major) # 4 print(v.minor) # 2 print(v.patch) # 0 print(v.prerelease) # "rc.1" print(v.build_metadata) # "sha.md5.f9300dc53" print(v.is_prerelease()) # true print(v.as_text()) # "4.2.0-rc.1+sha.md5.f9300dc53"
var a = SemVer.parse_semver("1.2.3") var b = SemVer.parse_semver("1.2.3-rc.1") print(a.gt(b)) # true - prerelease versions sort below their stable counterpart [/codeblock]

method.name: as_tag
method.description: Returns a string suitable for use as a release tag. For [SemVer] this is identical to [method as_text]; subclasses such as [GodotVer] override this to produce a different format.

method.name: as_text
method.description: Returns the canonical SemVer 2.0 string for this version, in the form [code]"MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH[-PRERELEASE][+BUILDMETADATA]"[/code]. Returns an empty string when [method is_valid_semver] is [code]false[/code].

method.name: create_semver
method.description: Constructs a [SemVer] directly from individual components. [param prerelease] is the dot-separated identifier list that follows the [code]-[/code] in a version string (e.g. [code]"rc.1"[/code], [code]"alpha.2"[/code]); pass an empty string for stable releases. [param build_metadata] is the dot-separated identifier list that follows the [code]+[/code] (e.g. [code]"sha.f9300dc53"[/code]); per the SemVer specification it does [i]not[/i] participate in version precedence.

method.name: eq
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when this version compares equal to [param other] under SemVer precedence rules. [member build_metadata] is ignored, so [code]"1.2.3+a"[/code] and [code]"1.2.3+b"[/code] compare equal.

method.name: gt
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when this version is strictly greater than [param other] under SemVer precedence rules.

method.name: gte
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when this version is greater than or equal to [param other] under SemVer precedence rules.

method.name: is_prerelease
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when [member prerelease] is non-empty - i.e. this version carries a pre-release tag such as [code]"alpha"[/code], [code]"beta.2"[/code], or [code]"rc.1"[/code].

method.name: is_valid_semver
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when this [SemVer] holds a successfully parsed or constructed version. Newly default-constructed instances are invalid until parsed or until any setter is called; instances returned by [method parse_semver] and [method create_semver] are always valid.

method.name: lt
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when this version is strictly less than [param other] under SemVer precedence rules.

method.name: lte
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when this version is less than or equal to [param other] under SemVer precedence rules.

method.name: neq
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when this version does not compare equal to [param other] under SemVer precedence rules. Equivalent to [code]not eq(other)[/code].

method.name: parse_semver
method.description: Parses a strict SemVer 2.0 version string and returns a [SemVer] instance. Accepts an optional leading [code]v[/code] or [code]V[/code]. Examples: [code]"1.2.3"[/code], [code]"1.1.3-pre.1"[/code], [code]"4.2.0-rc.1+sha.md5.f9300dc53"[/code]. Returns [code]null[/code] when [param ver_text] is not a valid SemVer string; for non-strict Godot version strings such as [code]"4.3.stable.official.77dcf97d8"[/code], use [method GodotVer.parse_godotver] instead.

members.member: Optional build-metadata identifier list that follows the [code]+[/code] in a SemVer string (e.g. [code]"sha.f9300dc53"[/code]). Per the SemVer 2.0 specification, build metadata is ignored when determining version precedence, so it does not affect the result of [method eq], [method lt], [method gt], or any other comparison method on this class.
The major version number. Incremented when incompatible API changes are made.
The minor version number. Incremented when functionality is added in a backwards-compatible manner.
The patch version number. Incremented for backwards-compatible bug fixes.

Optional dot-separated pre-release identifier list that follows the [code]-[/code] in a SemVer string (e.g. [code]"rc.1"[/code], [code]"alpha"[/code], [code]"beta.2"[/code]). A non-empty value indicates a pre-release version that sorts [i]below[/i] the corresponding stable release.
